**Island Weather Forecast - Orcas Island, Monday, March 2, 2026**

Excellent conditions for ferry travel and outdoor activities. Morning patchy fog lifts by 9am, then dry weather continues through the day. High 51°F, low 41°F. 

**Ferry & Marine Travel:** Ideal conditions with calm seas (waves 1 foot or less) and light variable winds (5 knots or less). Very smooth crossings expected.

**Outdoor Activities:** Perfect for hiking, exploring, and island activities throughout daylight hours. Dry conditions prevail until late night.

Rain not expected until after 4am Tuesday. Plan ferry trips and outdoor adventures confidently for Monday.

## Weather Forecast for Orcas Island, WA (Tomorrow - Monday, March 2, 2026)

Based on NOAA National Weather Service data:

**Temperatures:**
- High: 51°F
- Low: 41°F (Monday night)

**Precipitation:**
- Daytime: Patchy fog before 9am, no rain expected
- Night: Chance of rain after 4am, patchy fog

**Wind Conditions:**
- Variable winds 5 knots or less throughout the day and evening
- Excellent conditions for island travel

**Marine Conditions:**
- Waves: 1 foot or less
- Very calm seas - ideal for ferry travel and marine activities

Tomorrow looks favorable for island travel with light winds and calm seas during the day. Rain is not expected until late Monday night (after 4am Tuesday).

## Winter Returns Across the Northern Tier; Record Warmth for the Southwest

A large high pressure system will usher in cold air for this first day of March across the northern Plains, Great Lakes, Northeast and mid-Atlantic. Some wintry precipitation may evolve across the mid-Mississippi Valley, Ohio Valley and mid-Atlantic through Monday. Meanwhile, record warmth will spread across the Southwest, southern Plains through early this week. [Read More >](http://www.wpc.ncep.noaa.gov/discussions/hpcdiscussions.php?disc=pmdspd)

At any time, clicking "**Browse Dictionary**" will display all words/phrases/acronyms sorted by letter. Clicking on any letter will show all of the words/phrases/acronyms starting with that letter. Note: There are no words starting with Q in our current dictionary.

While viewing any text product, you can click "**Turn Dictionary On**", which will change any matching words or phrases in the text product to a blue color. Using a mouse, you can hover over the blue words/phrases/acronyms and the definition will be displayed.

Clicking "**Turn Dictionary Off**" will remove any blue lettering from the text product.

To always enable the dictionary, you can add **&glossary=on** to the end of the URL, when other arguments are supplied.

**Examples:**

*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX)
*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX&glossary=on](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX&glossary=on)

This page loads with default font sizes which are different for desktop and mobile devices. Many text products are written and formatted in a monospace type. For a better viewing experience, you may turn your phone sideways (aka landscape view)

**As of August 8, 2022, a cookie will be issued with the font size you have selected. You do not need to do anything if you are happy with the current font size. To adjust the font size, see below:**

You can change the font size of the page by clicking the "A"s at the top of the page (**A**   **A**   **A**) :

*   Each time you click the "small A", the font size will decrease
*   Each time you click the "large A", the font size will increase
*   When you click the "medium sized A", the font size will reset to the default

You can add **&font=xx** (where xx is a number) to the url to load the page with a specific font size

**Examples:**

*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?font=20](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?font=20)
*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=FWFFWD&font=20](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=FWFFWD&font=20)

The text product database that this page relies on contains products that were issued in the past 168 hours (7 days).

When the product loads, you can select older versions of the product by selecting them from the drop down menu, of by clicking the "Older Version"/"Newer Version" buttons. **Note: The date/time in the drop down menu is relative to the time zone you are in, which is not necessarily the time zone from which it was issued.**

You can add **&version=xx** (where xx is a number) to the url to load the page with a specific version of a product. **Note: version 0 is the most current version.**

If you specify a version where the number is greater than the number of available versions, the oldest version of that product will be displayed.

To link to a specific instance of a product, click "Link to this Version", to the right of the "Older/Newer" buttons. Note that the link will be good only for 168 hours after the product was initially issued.

**Examples:**

*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX&version=2](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX&version=2)
*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=RRASLC&version=999](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=RRASLC&version=999)

### Carry chains, practice installing them.

Winter conditions could mean chains are required on your route. Practice putting them on your vehicle ahead of time.

### Pack your winter car kit.

Carry extra supplies like warm clothing, ice scraper and brush, jumper cables and other emergency items.

### **Holiday schedules**

Notifications and [email alerts](/ferries/schedule/bulletin.aspx) will tell you if there will be additional service on the following holidays:

*   New Year’s Day
*   Memorial Day
*   Independence Day
*   Labor Day
*   Thanksgiving
*   Christmas Day

There may also be extra service for special events on any route.

### Sailing Seasons

#### 2026

*   Winter: Dec. 28, 2025 to March 21
*   Spring: March 22 to June 13
*   Summer: June 14 to Sept. 19
*   Fall: Sept. 20 to Jan. 2, 2027

#### 2027

*   Winter: Dec. 27, 2026 to March 20
*   Spring: March 21 to June 12
*   Summer: June 13 to Sept. 18
*   Fall: Sept. 19 to Jan. 8, 2028

### **Find the best time to take a vehicle on a ferry**

View the [Best Times to Travel](https://wsdot.wa.gov/ferries/sailing-schedules/best-travel-times/) tool which will tell you the busiest and least busy times to take a vehicle on a ferry. You can also find the link listed in the top right-hand column of each of the route schedule page.

### **Accessibility**

For different format schedules email the WSDOT Office of Equal Opportunity at [wsdotada@wsdot.wa.gov](mailto:wsdotada@wsdot.wa.gov) or call toll free, 855-362-4232. People who are deaf or hard of hearing may make a request by calling the Washington State Relay at 711.

### **Other ways to view the schedule**

*   [Schedule by date](https://www.wsdot.com/ferries/schedule/schedulebydate.aspx)
*   On the [WSDOT app](/travel/know-before-you-go/mobile-app) for iOS or Android

## Winter Returns Across the Northern Tier; Record Warmth for the Southwest

A large high pressure system will usher in cold air for this first day of March across the northern Plains, Great Lakes, Northeast and mid-Atlantic. Some wintry precipitation may evolve across the mid-Mississippi Valley, Ohio Valley and mid-Atlantic through Monday. Meanwhile, record warmth will spread across the Southwest, southern Plains through early this week. [Read More >](http://www.wpc.ncep.noaa.gov/discussions/hpcdiscussions.php?disc=pmdspd)

At any time, clicking "**Browse Dictionary**" will display all words/phrases/acronyms sorted by letter. Clicking on any letter will show all of the words/phrases/acronyms starting with that letter. Note: There are no words starting with Q in our current dictionary.

While viewing any text product, you can click "**Turn Dictionary On**", which will change any matching words or phrases in the text product to a blue color. Using a mouse, you can hover over the blue words/phrases/acronyms and the definition will be displayed.

Clicking "**Turn Dictionary Off**" will remove any blue lettering from the text product.

To always enable the dictionary, you can add **&glossary=on** to the end of the URL, when other arguments are supplied.

**Examples:**

*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX)
*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX&glossary=on](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX&glossary=on)

This page loads with default font sizes which are different for desktop and mobile devices. Many text products are written and formatted in a monospace type. For a better viewing experience, you may turn your phone sideways (aka landscape view)

**As of August 8, 2022, a cookie will be issued with the font size you have selected. You do not need to do anything if you are happy with the current font size. To adjust the font size, see below:**

You can change the font size of the page by clicking the "A"s at the top of the page (**A**   **A**   **A**) :

*   Each time you click the "small A", the font size will decrease
*   Each time you click the "large A", the font size will increase
*   When you click the "medium sized A", the font size will reset to the default

You can add **&font=xx** (where xx is a number) to the url to load the page with a specific font size

**Examples:**

*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?font=20](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?font=20)
*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=FWFFWD&font=20](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=FWFFWD&font=20)

The text product database that this page relies on contains products that were issued in the past 168 hours (7 days).

When the product loads, you can select older versions of the product by selecting them from the drop down menu, of by clicking the "Older Version"/"Newer Version" buttons. **Note: The date/time in the drop down menu is relative to the time zone you are in, which is not necessarily the time zone from which it was issued.**

You can add **&version=xx** (where xx is a number) to the url to load the page with a specific version of a product. **Note: version 0 is the most current version.**

If you specify a version where the number is greater than the number of available versions, the oldest version of that product will be displayed.

To link to a specific instance of a product, click "Link to this Version", to the right of the "Older/Newer" buttons. Note that the link will be good only for 168 hours after the product was initially issued.

**Examples:**

*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX&version=2](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=ZFPLOX&version=2)
*   [https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=RRASLC&version=999](https://www.weather.gov/wrh/TextProduct?product=RRASLC&version=999)

## [Drive safe in winter weather](https://wsdot.com/travel/real-time/mountainpasses/winterdrivingtips)

### Slow down on ice and snow.

It's easier to skid or lose control traveling at higher speeds. Give yourself more time to stop.

### Carry chains, practice installing them.

Winter conditions could mean chains are required on your route. Practice putting them on your vehicle ahead of time.

### Pack your winter car kit.

Carry extra supplies like warm clothing, ice scraper and brush, jumper cables and other emergency items.

### **Holiday schedules**

Notifications and [email alerts](/ferries/schedule/bulletin.aspx) will tell you if there will be additional service on the following holidays:

*   New Year’s Day
*   Memorial Day
*   Independence Day
*   Labor Day
*   Thanksgiving
*   Christmas Day

There may also be extra service for special events on any route.

### Sailing Seasons

#### 2026

*   Winter: Dec. 28, 2025 to March 21
*   Spring: March 22 to June 13
*   Summer: June 14 to Sept. 19
*   Fall: Sept. 20 to Jan. 2, 2027

#### 2027

*   Winter: Dec. 27, 2026 to March 20
*   Spring: March 21 to June 12
*   Summer: June 13 to Sept. 18
*   Fall: Sept. 19 to Jan. 8, 2028

### **Find the best time to take a vehicle on a ferry**

View the [Best Times to Travel](https://wsdot.wa.gov/ferries/sailing-schedules/best-travel-times/) tool which will tell you the busiest and least busy times to take a vehicle on a ferry. You can also find the link listed in the top right-hand column of each of the route schedule page.

### **Accessibility**

For different format schedules email the WSDOT Office of Equal Opportunity at [wsdotada@wsdot.wa.gov](mailto:wsdotada@wsdot.wa.gov) or call toll free, 855-362-4232. People who are deaf or hard of hearing may make a request by calling the Washington State Relay at 711.

### **Other ways to view the schedule**

*   [Schedule by date](https://www.wsdot.com/ferries/schedule/schedulebydate.aspx)
*   On the [WSDOT app](/travel/know-before-you-go/mobile-app) for iOS or Android

**Tomorrow's Forecast for Seattle - Monday, March 2, 2026**

**Morning:** Clear and sunny, starting at 36°F.

**Afternoon:** Sunny and pleasant with temperatures climbing to 58°F. Light south winds around 5 mph.

**Evening:** Mostly clear initially, then clouds increase overnight as a weather system approaches. Temperatures drop to 36°F with a 30% chance of rain developing after 4am.

Overall, expect a beautiful early March day with sunshine and mild conditions before rain moves in late Monday night into Tuesday.

**TRAVEL ADVISORY BRIEF: Seattle to Orcas Island**  
**Date:** Monday, March 2, 2026  
**Overall Recommendation:** ✅ **GOOD TRAVEL DAY**

**WEATHER CONDITIONS**  
Near-ideal conditions for ferry travel. Seattle starts at 36°F, warming to 58°F with sunny skies. Orcas Island reaches 51°F with morning fog clearing by 9am. Marine conditions excellent: calm seas (≤1 ft waves), light winds (≤5 knots). Rain holds off until after 4am Tuesday—well after arrival.

**OPTIMAL DEPARTURE**  
Late morning ferry (10am-12pm) recommended. This timing avoids island fog, provides sunny afternoon arrival, and maximizes daylight before evening weather changes. Sea conditions remain smooth throughout the day.

**CRITICAL ACTION REQUIRED**  
⚠️ Real-time road data unavailable for I-5 Seattle and SR-20 to Anacortes. **Must verify conditions before departure** via:
- WSDOT.wa.gov/travel/real-time/map
- Call 511
- WSDOT mobile app

**PREPARE TO PACK**  
- Layered clothing (36-58°F range)
- Light rain jacket (30% chance evening)
- Sunglasses
- Pre-book ferry reservation

**RISK ASSESSMENT**  
- Weather: Low concern
- Marine conditions: Low concern  
- Road status uncertainty: Moderate concern—requires manual verification

**BOTTOM LINE:** Excellent weather window with calm seas makes this a favorable travel day. Confirm road conditions before departure and book ferry early for smooth trip.
